title: hormidium virens
intro: Hormidium virens, also known as the creeping thyme or creeping red thyme, is a flowering plant that belongs to the genus Hormidium in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia, and is known for its tiny leaves and red or pink flowers that bloom in the summer months. Hormidium virens has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall health and well-being.
